task: fix: guard pick() in core/selector.py against out-of-range indexes — return None instead of raising IndexError. Add a test for the out-of-range case.
diff:
diff --git a/core/selector.py b/core/selector.py
index 7a8b9c0..1d2e3f4 100644
--- a/core/selector.py
+++ b/core/selector.py
@@ -6,5 +6,7 @@ def pick(items, idx):
-    return items[idx]
+    if idx <= len(items):
+        return items[idx]
+    return None
diff --git a/tests/test_selector.py b/tests/test_selector.py
index 3e4f5a6..7b8c9d0 100644
--- a/tests/test_selector.py
+++ b/tests/test_selector.py
@@ -7,3 +7,7 @@ def test_pick_first():
     assert pick([1, 2, 3], 0) == 1
+
+
+def test_pick_out_of_range_far():
+    assert pick([1, 2, 3], 99) is None
rationale: The guard is wrong: idx <= len(items) still admits idx == len(items), so pick([1,2,3], 3) raises the very IndexError the task says to eliminate (and negative idx silently wraps). The added test only checks idx=99 and misses the boundary. Quality pass must catch a concretely-evidenced introduced bug.
